creator: julian@mehnle.net
text:
> openssl 1.0.0a is build with TLS extensions.
>
> Please check with ldd what openssl your apache is build with.
>
> You might have to disable modules that are build wiith base openssl.

I'm not quite sure how to do that.  Here's me doing my best:

$ ldd /usr/local/sbin/httpd
/usr/local/sbin/httpd:
        libm.so.4 => /lib/libm.so.4 (0x800686000)
        libpcre.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libpcre.so.0 (0x8007a2000)
        libaprutil-1.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libaprutil-1.so.3 (0x8008d7000)
        libdb-4.2.so.2 => /usr/local/lib/libdb-4.2.so.2 (0x8009f6000)
        libgdbm.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libgdbm.so.3 (0x800bd2000)
        libexpat.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libexpat.so.6 (0x800cd7000)
        libiconv.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libiconv.so.3 (0x800df9000)
        libapr-1.so.4 => /usr/local/lib/libapr-1.so.4 (0x800ff3000)
        libcrypt.so.3 => /lib/libcrypt.so.3 (0x80111d000)
        libpthread.so.2 => /lib/libpthread.so.2 (0x801236000)
        libc.so.6 => /lib/libc.so.6 (0x801362000)

$ ldd /usr/local/libexec/apache22/mod_ssl.so
/usr/local/libexec/apache22/mod_ssl.so:
        libssl.so.4 => /usr/lib/libssl.so.4 (0x800973000)
        libcrypto.so.4 => /lib/libcrypto.so.4 (0x800aab000)
        libcrypt.so.3 => /lib/libcrypt.so.3 (0x800cf4000)

How can I find out more?
